children.title: A Minto Pyramid deserves a dedicated workspace
Time: You value the audience's time by getting to the point
Intellect: You trust the audience to get the big picture
Judgement: You trust the audience to determine how much detail they need

Clarity — strive for precise arguments and robust logic
Ownership — prepare for exposing your findings to scrutiny
Focus — look for the key finding
It doesn’t translate well to PowerPoint
Minto Pyramid gives power to the audience, PowerPoint keeps it with the presenter
Minto Pyramid
children.body:
”Where do you want to go next?”
”How do you want to spend the next ten minutes?”
”What do you want to explore in more detail?”

children.title: PowerPoint
children.body:
The presenter decides the order in advance  Audience input is difficult to incorporate into the sequence
Skipping in the presentation breaks the rythm

children.title: Minto Pyramid guides the thought where PowerPoint records thoughts
Minto Pyramid supports non-linear consumption of information

PowerPoint
PowerPoint supports linear storytelling
The Minto Pyramid is a layered structure, while PowerPoint follows a sequential logic
This
becomes
this
When forced into PowerPoint
”Is this relevant?”
”Does this support the claim?”
”Is the claim robust enough?”
”I can always decrease the font size”
”I can add a slide for this”
”This is an interesting piece of information”
Man bites dog
Minto Pyramid — Gives power to the audience — Is a layered structure — Guides the thought
PowerPoint — Keeps power with the presenter — Follows a sequential logic — Records thoughts
